data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money, having raised $616M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse) valued between $1.1B and $2.5B, alongside 5 public sector comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implies $1.7B. The peer-median method across 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields a median of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue data. These two methods converge closely, supporting a triangulated valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.7B.

The analysis carries material uncertainty around company-specific traction metrics and capital intensity requirements relative to peers. The $300M spread between low and high reflects legitimate variance in how stage progression and peer positioning are weighted; the clustering of both methods near $1.7B suggests reasonable confidence around the midpoint.
